title: OpenClaw should not crash on WhatsApp events that arrive after the active run ends

source.license_note: Synthetic ops scenario derived from openclaw/openclaw issue #62840.

task.summary: Diagnose why late WhatsApp listener events crash the gateway after the active run ends.
task.instructions: Use the incident evidence to confirm the late-listener crash, localize it to missing run guards around listener processing, and propose an ops-level repair plan that skips late events safely.
task.acceptance_notes: This GitHub-derived ops scenario is synthetic and graded behaviorally.

visible_context.issue_text: A WhatsApp message arriving after the previous run finishes can crash the gateway with `Agent listener invoked outside active run`.
error_logs: Unhandled promise rejection: Error: Agent listener invoked outside active run
gateway exited with status=1 after a new WhatsApp event arrived
reproduction_steps: Explain where the listener path should guard and skip post-run updates.
visible_context.notes: This fixture models listener-runtime incident triage rather than a code fixture.

ops_fault.description: Synthetic listener-runtime incident derived from the GitHub issue.

notes: Synthetic ops scenario derived from openclaw/openclaw issue #62840.
